recent_commits.body: This document will be helpful to be able to quickly find functions module developers or users may not be aware of, organized better than default roxygen output. Minor fix to extract_eq description, and added gitattributes to prevent changes fixtures due to line endings setting in windows.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: stat_effect_size: detect tests via extract_eq(); add implied sample sizes (#308)
recent_commits.body:
Replace the module's duplicate t/F/effect-size regex extraction with the
shared extract_eq() equation table, per #308. Detection now reads tests and
effect sizes from eq rows (one output row per test), removing ~315 lines of
duplicated extraction: the two text_search detection pipelines, the
expand_multi_*_rows helpers, and the now-unreachable multi-test window
branches in the coherence classifiers.

Also fixes the #308 warnings (early return when no tests are detected) and,
validated against the coded ground truth (0.0.1.0):
- F-tests again require a two-integer (df1, df2), matching the validated
  detection; bare "t = value" is kept as before.
- test_text no longer glues a literal "NA" when df is absent.

For d coherence matches, the coherence note now reports the sample sizes that
reproduce the reported effect size (n1 = n2, N for equal-n; closest n1, n2, N
for unequal-n) so users can manually verify; a d_implied_n column is added to
the table. Paired dz records n = df + 1 without extra note text.

Corpus parity at the individual-test level: 2257/2281 coded tests retained;
remaining differences are mostly fuller value parsing and correct row
splitting. Ground truth needs regenerating/recoding for the new row structure.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: Align reg_check module style with the other modules
recent_commits.body:
Use summarise(.by=) instead of group_by, bind_rows instead of rbind,
message() instead of warning() for the failure path, plain-text judgement
labels instead of emoji, plain bibentry names, and the standard dev-test
comment.

recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: Add reg_check module: RegCheck preregistration-paper comparison
recent_commits.body:
New module reg_check compares preregistrations (retrieved by prereg_check,
chained or run internally) with the paper via a RegCheck server, defaulting
to a local Ollama-based server so no text leaves the machine. Adds the
regcheck_compare() API client with latin-1-safe text sanitization, a
bachelor-level vignette, tests, and an osf_type() fix for empty input.

repos.readme_excerpt:
`statcheck` is a “spellchecker” for statistics. It checks whether your
*p*-values match their accompanying test statistic and degrees of
freedom.

`statcheck` searches for null-hypothesis significance test (NHST) in APA
style (e.g., *t*(28) = 2.2, *p* \< .05). It recalculates the p-value
using the reported test statistic and degrees of freedom. If the
reported and computed p-values don’t match, `statcheck` will flag the
result as an error.

![](man/figures/infograph.png)

1.  **Self-checks**: you can use `statcheck` to make sure your

recent_commits.message: feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark)
recent_commits.body:
The dominant defect on two-column APA papers: pdftotext serializes a page's
columns interleaved, scrambling Method/Results/Discussion order and splitting
paragraphs. The Step-1 whole-page corrector (v2.4.82) cannot reach these pages
-- its bilateral y-row gate and full-height gutter strip both reject any page
carrying a full-width band (table row / banner / wide title) crossing the column
centre, which is exactly the failing pages (TRIAGE 2026-06-15: GENERAL=1 was
byte-near-identical on these papers).

Step 2 (extract_page_text_banded, docpluck/extract_columns.py) segments a
flagged page into horizontal y-BANDS: a band whose central gutter strip is
glyph-free is two-column prose -> re-extracted left-then-right; a full-width band
(table / banner / title) is kept as-is. Bands reassemble top-to-bottom at
glyph-free cut lines (vertically-overlapping bands merge to full-width so a tall
title glyph is never bisected). Applied ONLY as a fallback inside
splice_column_corrected_pages when the whole-page path returns "", under the SAME
unconditional word-preservation guard -- a band crop that drops/fabricates a word
is rejected and the page kept as-is, so the flag can only ADD a pure reorder,
never ship corruption.

Gated behind DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_BANDED (default OFF, ship-dark): flag OFF is
byte-identical to v2.4.89. No NORMALIZATION_VERSION change (default path
unchanged; the flag acts upstream of normalize).

Validation (2026-06-15): corpus word-preservation scan (71 flagged pages, 4
papers) -- every accepted page is a pure reorder; the 6 residual band-cut clips
are all guard-rejected (no corruption). Production smoke flag-ON corrects ~44
pages across 5 papers, all word-safe; flag-OFF byte-identical. AI-verify vs
article-finder reading golds (Sonnet subagents) on chan_feldman_2025_cogemo +
chandrashekar_2023_mp: both ON_BETTER -- Procedure/Manipulations ordering and the
Power-analysis paragraph split fixed (chan); `## Results` heading restored and
column-token orphans suppressed (chandrashekar); 0 text-loss/hallucination/
regression vs flag OFF. Touched-path suite 302 passed; 26-paper baseline 26/26
(flag OFF).

New regression tests/test_rc1_banded_column_real_pdf.py (synthetic-layout
geometry units + real-PDF word-preservation + ship-dark-default). Remaining
Step-2 refinements (band-cut clips, per-row both-sides under-detection,
title+sidebar pages) documented in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-08-rc1-region-
aware-column-architecture.md for the eventual default-flip.

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.89): B7 W0h -- recover dropped-minus coefficients from the layout channel
recent_commits.body:
The no-CI residual W0g cannot reach: a body-prose coefficient with only a t/p
value (b = -.022, t(87) = .17) where pdftotext drops the U+2212 glyph entirely
on tight-kerned PDFs that draw the minus in a dedicated symbol font. The dropped
minus survives in the layout channel (pdfplumber) as an unmapped (cid:N) glyph
in the `<stat> = <minus><coef>` operator slot; recover_dropped_minus_via_layout
(W0h) re-inserts it.

- normalize.py: W0h + _layout_negative_coefficients (y-overlap line clustering,
  =-anchored coef slot, flips only layout-proven negatives, count-limited).
  NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.34 -> 1.9.35.
- Threaded a DEDICATED `dropped_minus_layout` param (render -> extract_sections
  -> normalize_text) so F0 stays off in the text-channel-only section path.
- ar_apa_j_jesp_2009_12_011: recovers b = -.022 / -.88 / -.428; leaves +.48.
  Surgical blast radius (only ar_apa flips; 4 other canary papers byte-identical).

Documented limitation: ar_apa b = -.245 is painted pixels (absent from pdftotext
AND pdfplumber chars/lines/rects/curves AND pdfminer raw layer) -> OCR-only, left
untouched rather than guessed. See TODO.md R5 Path 1.

recent_commits.message: test(canary): assert audit input PDF byte-equal to pinned feed (R-0003)
recent_commits.body:
Cross-project lesson transfer (CitationGuard => docpluck): the verification
substrate must be byte-equal to the production input feed before scoring. A
CitationGuard run once scored its tool output against raw PyMuPDF for a whole
run, producing a misleading score.

docpluck already scores render-vs-AI-gold (never a deterministic extractor --
CLAUDE.md hard rule), so the residual gap that lesson maps to here is INPUT
provenance: a render of a drifted PDF scored against a gold made from the
original is the same "wrong substrate" bug class. If the cached PDF for a DOI
ever drifts (re-download, re-encode, a stray Dropbox conflicted-copy), the
canary would silently score a render of a different input than the gold.

- tools/canary_provenance.py: load_expected_sha() + check_provenance() helpers
- canary.json: pin expected_pdf_sha for all 5 onboarded canary papers
- render_for_audit.py: --expected-sha (falls back to the canary pin); abort with
  exit 4 BEFORE rendering on mismatch; record expected_pdf_sha + provenance_ok
  in the per-render manifest. Un-pinned keys are a no-op (purely additive guard).
- tests/test_canary_provenance.py: 10 tests (helper units, 5 byte-equality
  integration vs the real cached PDFs, render-guard exit-4)

Gate: 10/10 new tests pass; full library suite 1710 passed / 0 failed (no
regression); live render smoke auto-loads the pin, matches, renders, exit 0.
The Sonnet canary itself is not runnable in this env (ai-gold get --view reading
errors + claude -p nesting hang); provenance is a pre-render guard that does not
change render output, so pytest is the precise gate.

recent_commits.body:
Root cause of the pre-push canary false-block ("No module named docpluck",
RENDER FAILED x5) was a Python-env issue, NOT a render regression: all deps
lived in the USER site-packages (AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314), invisible
under `python -s` / PYTHONNOUSERSITE -- the mode the git hook / headless render
subprocess runs in.

- .claude/skills/_project/canary.json: render_command `python` -> `py -3`.
- tools/fix_python_env.ps1: one-time elevated grant of Modify on
  C:\Python314\Lib\site-packages + Scripts; deps reinstalled into the GLOBAL
  site (verified importable under `python -s`). Future pip installs now land in
  the global site automatically -> no recurrence.
- TODO.md: record the v2.4.86/87/88 push + the Python consolidation.

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.86/87/88): ScienceArena re-audit — span x-gap spacing, F0 body-from-text-channel, camelot Windows table fix
recent_commits.body:
ScienceArena's public PDF leaderboard showed docpluck below GROBID (sections,
tables) and below GROBID+liteparse (text). Two findings:
  (1) GROBID-beats-docpluck is an ARENA artifact — leaderboard.py::aggregate()
      flat-means over asymmetric task sets (GROBID ran only the synthetic
      subset; on common tasks docpluck >= all). Handed back to ScienceArena via
      HANDOFF_2026-06-13_pdf_arena_global_fairness.md (fix globally at the
      framework layer, all players + all arenas).
  (2) liteparse/pdftotext beating docpluck on real PMC text fidelity was a REAL
      docpluck bug — fixed here.

v2.4.86 — extract_layout._join_chars_with_spaces reinserts inter-word spaces
  from the x-gap. pdfplumber's char stream drops the space glyph on tight-kerned
  PDFs (Cambridge / two-column), so span text glued words ("CNSSpectrums"); the
  F0 layout body then collapsed to token-F1 ~0.00 on 16/30 PMC papers. (LESSONS L-007)

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.84): R2 quantifier-head guard — stop deleting digits from reference titles
recent_commits.body:
CitationGuard handoff 2026-06-10 item 4: plos_med_1 "Clinimetric properties of
3 instruments" rendered as "of instruments" — the standalone "3" was silently
deleted. Root cause is docpluck's own R2 page-number scrub (normalize.py), NOT
pdftotext (raw extracts "of 3 instruments" correctly): R2 strips any digit whose
value also appears as a standalone page-number line when it sits between two
lowercase words. "3" is page 3, so the quantifier "3 instruments" was stripped.
The v2.4.17 body-noun allowlist never enumerated "instruments" — whack-a-mole.

Fix keys on a closed class instead (NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.30 -> 1.9.31): a
genuine page leak follows a CONTENT word ("psychological 41 science"); a
legitimate quantifier follows a FUNCTION word ("of 3 instruments", "the 5
factors", "and 3 in"). _r2_is_body_phrase now also preserves a digit whose
immediately-preceding token is in _R2_QUANTIFIER_HEAD_WORDS. Purely additive —
only ever preserves, never strips more — honoring the rule-0a asymmetry
(dropping a digit from a reference title is far worse than a stray page number).

Corpus audit (101 PDFs, academic-normalized, old vs new): 5 papers changed, all
correct restorations, 0 regressions. Repaired the filed defect PLUS 4
pre-existing silent drops: amp_1 "12 academic search engines", bmc_med_3 "5
types", ieee_access_5 "3 x 3" + "30 iterations", maier_2023 "Experiments 1a and
3". The 11 remaining R2 strips (all content-word-preceded) are genuine leaks,
correctly still stripped.

Handoff items 1-3 (chen_2021_jesp: lost "Registered reports:" prefix, "TurkPrime.
Com" space injection, "Open, S. C." author mangle) are WON'T-FIX: verified
byte-identical in BOTH pdftotext and pdfplumber -> PDF-embedded-text-layer

- NormalizationReport.footnote_texts: the captured footnote strings, parallel

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.82): column-splice word-integrity (pre-existing corruption) + RC-1 Step1 general de-interleave (flag, dark)
recent_commits.body:
Reproducing the RC-1 Step-1 gap at HEAD surfaced that the v2.4.81 DEFAULT was
corrupting words on 5/26 baseline papers via the column-splice. Two latent bugs
in splice_column_corrected_pages (both since R4 v2.4.76):

- accept-any word SPLITS: pdftotext column-crop cut words straddling the crop-x;
  pages outside the O5 word-preserve set accepted them unchecked (jama_open_1
  adults->adu, jama_open_2 creatinine->cre, ieee_access_3 using->us, amc_1
  management->mana+agement, amle_1). The committed snapshots jama_lattice +
  ieee_figure_heavy were STORING the corruption (broken baseline masking it).
- cross-page boundary GLUE: extract_page_text_columns returns page text stripped
  of its trailing form-feed; the splice appended it directly, so a corrected
  page's last word glued onto the next page's first word (bjps_1
  results+https->resultshttps; running-header J->fromj / scienceJ). The per-page
  guard passed because each page was word-correct in isolation.

Fix: word-preservation is now UNCONDITIONAL (a corrected page is accepted only
as a pure reorder — identical substantial-word multiset AND a different token
order) and the original page's trailing newline+form-feed separator is
re-attached so no word glues across the page boundary. Param word_preserve_pages
-> gutter_fallback_pages (now governs only the gutter-strip midline detector;
word-preservation applies to every page).

Validated: all 26 baseline papers preserve the raw pdftotext word-multiset under
DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_GENERAL both off and on (was 7 corrupted). Full suite
1892 passed / 27 skipped / 1 xfailed.

RC-1 Step 1: the general two-column-interleave flagged pages (previously
signal-only) are wired to the splice behind DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_GENERAL
(default OFF -> byte-identical, ships dark). AI-verified vs article-finder golds:
j.jesp.2021.104154 numbered-section inversions 12+ -> 4; ip_feldman B3
affiliation-leak + B4 mid-text-caption canary findings cleared. Partial
(table-bearing / no-clean-gutter pages stay untouched -> Step 2), hence dark
pending the per-band region-aware crop.

Consequence (rule 0a/0b word-integrity > reading order): R4's jama_open_1
abstract de-interleave was built on the splitting crop; word-preservation now
rejects it, reverting that page to word-correct-but-column-mixed. Proper
de-interleave of that full-width-title page needs Step 2. test_r4_* revised to
assert word-integrity (whole words present, split fragments absent).

Tests: tests/test_rc1_general_column_correction_real_pdf.py (new, real-PDF,
flag-off legacy + flag-on corrects-more with whole-doc multiset preserved);
tests/test_r4_column_correction_real_pdf.py (word-integrity revision); snapshots
jama_lattice / ieee_figure_heavy / apa_chen_jesp_lineless regenerated to
corrected (word-preserving) output.

Run verdict PARTIAL: canary still FAILs on known-deferred Step-2 (region-aware
band) + two separable residuals queued (bare 'J. Chen et al.' running-header
leak; collabra M_age 59.3->39.3 glyph misread).

design; the whole-page shortcut is a dead end. Live extract_columns.py left UNCHANGED (clean

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.79): clear canary findings #1 (METADATA-LEAK) + #2 (HALLUCINATION false-positive) on ip_feldman + canary-gate substrate fixes
recent_commits.body:
Cycle 5. NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.27 -> 1.9.28. Both library fixes keyed on a
structural signature, never paper identity. Gated: full pytest 1870 passed,
26/26 corpus baseline, canary audit confirmed zero METADATA-LEAK / HALLUCINATION
findings remain on ip_feldman.

Finding #1 - US-format publication-history line strip (normalize.py
_PAGE_FOOTER_LINE_PATTERNS): strips "Received Month DD, YYYY; revision accepted
Month DD, YYYY" (Sage/APA - PSPB) leaking between ## Keywords and ## Introduction.
Existing patterns are European-order; this adds US "Month DD, YYYY" order + the
distinctive "revision accepted" phrase (date sub-pattern accepts either order;
"revision accepted" + trailing year = unmistakable journal boilerplate).

Finding #2 - continuation-demoter rejoin (render.py
_demote_continuation_promoted_headings): the demoted continuation is rejoined to
the prior line it grammatically continues (terminal period restored when
sentence-final) instead of left as an orphan bare line. "...summarized in
the\n\nSupplemental Materials" now renders as one continuous sentence matching
gold line 86. Confirmed audit false-positive (text always real); the defect was
the spurious split. Completes the rejoin the docstring had deferred.

Canary-gate substrate (shared ~/.claude/skills/_shared/iterate-loop, not in this
repo's history but recorded here):
- finding_key line-number-invariance: _norm_location no longer embeds the line
  number (any render change that shifts lines was falsely re-opening deferred
  findings as NEW, defeating --gate-new-only). Identity now rests on severity +
  verbatim excerpt. + invariance self-test (17/17).
- allowed_omissions: new optional audit-payload field so the Sonnet canary
  auditor does not flag content the library omits BY DESIGN (front-matter

_new_commits.message: feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark)
_new_commits.body:
The dominant defect on two-column APA papers: pdftotext serializes a page's
columns interleaved, scrambling Method/Results/Discussion order and splitting
paragraphs. The Step-1 whole-page corrector (v2.4.82) cannot reach these pages
-- its bilateral y-row gate and full-height gutter strip both reject any page
carrying a full-width band (table row / banner / wide title) crossing the column
centre, which is exactly the failing pages (TRIAGE 2026-06-15: GENERAL=1 was
byte-near-identical on these papers).

Step 2 (extract_page_text_banded, docpluck/extract_columns.py) segments a
flagged page into horizontal y-BANDS: a band whose central gutter strip is
glyph-free is two-column prose -> re-extracted left-then-right; a full-width band
(table / banner / title) is kept as-is. Bands reassemble top-to-bottom at
glyph-free cut lines (vertically-overlapping bands merge to full-width so a tall
title glyph is never bisected). Applied ONLY as a fallback inside
splice_column_corrected_pages when the whole-page path returns "", under the SAME
unconditional word-preservation guard -- a band crop that drops/fabricates a word
is rejected and the page kept as-is, so the flag can only ADD a pure reorder,
never ship corruption.

Gated behind DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_BANDED (default OFF, ship-dark): flag OFF is
byte-identical to v2.4.89. No NORMALIZATION_VERSION change (default path
unchanged; the flag acts upstream of normalize).

Validation (2026-06-15): corpus word-preservation scan (71 flagged pages, 4
papers) -- every accepted page is a pure reorder; the 6 residual band-cut clips
are all guard-rejected (no corruption). Production smoke flag-ON corrects ~44
pages across 5 papers, all word-safe; flag-OFF byte-identical. AI-verify vs
article-finder reading golds (Sonnet subagents) on chan_feldman_2025_cogemo +
chandrashekar_2023_mp: both ON_BETTER -- Procedure/Manipulations ordering and the
Power-analysis paragraph split fixed (chan); `## Results` heading restored and
column-token orphans suppressed (chandrashekar); 0 text-loss/hallucination/
regression vs flag OFF. Touched-path suite 302 passed; 26-paper baseline 26/26
(flag OFF).

New regression tests/test_rc1_banded_column_real_pdf.py (synthetic-layout
geometry units + real-PDF word-preservation + ship-dark-default). Remaining
Step-2 refinements (band-cut clips, per-row both-sides under-detection,
title+sidebar pages) documented in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-08-rc1-region-
aware-column-architecture.md for the eventual default-flip.

_new_commits.message: fix(v2.4.89): B7 W0h -- recover dropped-minus coefficients from the layout channel
_new_commits.body:
The no-CI residual W0g cannot reach: a body-prose coefficient with only a t/p
value (b = -.022, t(87) = .17) where pdftotext drops the U+2212 glyph entirely
on tight-kerned PDFs that draw the minus in a dedicated symbol font. The dropped
minus survives in the layout channel (pdfplumber) as an unmapped (cid:N) glyph
in the `<stat> = <minus><coef>` operator slot; recover_dropped_minus_via_layout
(W0h) re-inserts it.

- normalize.py: W0h + _layout_negative_coefficients (y-overlap line clustering,
  =-anchored coef slot, flips only layout-proven negatives, count-limited).
  NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.34 -> 1.9.35.
- Threaded a DEDICATED `dropped_minus_layout` param (render -> extract_sections
  -> normalize_text) so F0 stays off in the text-channel-only section path.
- ar_apa_j_jesp_2009_12_011: recovers b = -.022 / -.88 / -.428; leaves +.48.
  Surgical blast radius (only ar_apa flips; 4 other canary papers byte-identical).

Documented limitation: ar_apa b = -.245 is painted pixels (absent from pdftotext
AND pdfplumber chars/lines/rects/curves AND pdfminer raw layer) -> OCR-only, left
untouched rather than guessed. See TODO.md R5 Path 1.

_new_commits.message: test(canary): assert audit input PDF byte-equal to pinned feed (R-0003)
_new_commits.body:
Cross-project lesson transfer (CitationGuard => docpluck): the verification
substrate must be byte-equal to the production input feed before scoring. A
CitationGuard run once scored its tool output against raw PyMuPDF for a whole
run, producing a misleading score.

docpluck already scores render-vs-AI-gold (never a deterministic extractor --
CLAUDE.md hard rule), so the residual gap that lesson maps to here is INPUT
provenance: a render of a drifted PDF scored against a gold made from the
original is the same "wrong substrate" bug class. If the cached PDF for a DOI
ever drifts (re-download, re-encode, a stray Dropbox conflicted-copy), the
canary would silently score a render of a different input than the gold.

- tools/canary_provenance.py: load_expected_sha() + check_provenance() helpers
- canary.json: pin expected_pdf_sha for all 5 onboarded canary papers
- render_for_audit.py: --expected-sha (falls back to the canary pin); abort with
  exit 4 BEFORE rendering on mismatch; record expected_pdf_sha + provenance_ok
  in the per-render manifest. Un-pinned keys are a no-op (purely additive guard).
- tests/test_canary_provenance.py: 10 tests (helper units, 5 byte-equality
  integration vs the real cached PDFs, render-guard exit-4)

Gate: 10/10 new tests pass; full library suite 1710 passed / 0 failed (no
regression); live render smoke auto-loads the pin, matches, renders, exit 0.
The Sonnet canary itself is not runnable in this env (ai-gold get --view reading
errors + claude -p nesting hang); provenance is a pre-render guard that does not
change render output, so pytest is the precise gate.

Only PDF sources run Mistral OCR; .tex/.md/.docx/.html/.epub/.txt extract
locally and the literature search falls back to the free arXiv path, so a
non-PDF "Review with my subscription" run needs no OpenRouter key anywhere.
The mechanics already supported this end-to-end, but every user-facing
surface demanded a key unconditionally — worst, buildAgentPrompt STEP 2
made the coding agent stop a key-free .tex review to ask for a key it
would never use.

  needed, do not ask for one"

  branch on it

recent_commits.message: fix(cost): recalibrate reasoning-model cost estimate; default Qwen 3.7 Plus (#212) (#213)
recent_commits.body:
The pre-flight cost estimate over-quoted reasoning models badly (GPT-5.5
~$81 for a 30k-token paper). Prices were correct; the inflation was two
compounding conservatisms — per-stage output budgets used the agents'
max_tokens ceilings (e.g. section=10000) instead of expected output, and
reasoning models multiplied output ×5 (reasoningOverheadMultiplier=4).

Recalibrate:
- pipeline_spec.STAGE_OUTPUT_TOKENS trimmed to expected output for the
  review-model stages (section 10000->4000, proof_verify 16384->6000,
  editorial 24000->12000, overview 8192->4096, completeness 4096->3072,
  cross_section 8192->4096). Cost-estimate-only — does NOT bound runtime
  max_tokens, so real outputs are unaffected.
- Reasoning overhead multiplier 4 -> 1.5 in both pipeline_spec (web, via
  regenerated pipelineSpec.json) and llm._REASONING_OVERHEAD_MULTIPLIER
  (Python gate), kept in lockstep.

GPT-5.5 now estimates ~$20. Trade-off: a slimmer never-under-quote margin
(chosen deliberately). Also bumps the model picker default Qwen 3.6 -> 3.7
Plus (qwen/qwen3.7-plus, verified on OpenRouter). Coverage in test_cost.py.

recent_commits.message: fix(cost): recalibrate reasoning-model cost estimate; default Qwen 3.7 Plus (#212)
recent_commits.body:
The pre-flight cost estimate over-quoted reasoning models badly (GPT-5.5
~$81 for a 30k-token paper). Prices were correct; the inflation was two
compounding conservatisms — per-stage output budgets used the agents'
max_tokens ceilings (e.g. section=10000) instead of expected output, and
reasoning models multiplied output ×5 (reasoningOverheadMultiplier=4).

Recalibrate:
- pipeline_spec.STAGE_OUTPUT_TOKENS trimmed to expected output for the
  review-model stages (section 10000->4000, proof_verify 16384->6000,
  editorial 24000->12000, overview 8192->4096, completeness 4096->3072,
  cross_section 8192->4096). Cost-estimate-only — does NOT bound runtime
  max_tokens, so real outputs are unaffected.
- Reasoning overhead multiplier 4 -> 1.5 in both pipeline_spec (web, via
  regenerated pipelineSpec.json) and llm._REASONING_OVERHEAD_MULTIPLIER
  (Python gate), kept in lockstep.

GPT-5.5 now estimates ~$20. Trade-off: a slimmer never-under-quote margin
(chosen deliberately). Also bumps the model picker default Qwen 3.6 -> 3.7
Plus (qwen/qwen3.7-plus, verified on OpenRouter). Coverage in test_cost.py.

recent_commits.message: feat(web): per-comment Discuss chat streaming directly to OpenRouter (#209) (#210)
recent_commits.body:
* feat(web): per-comment Discuss chat streaming directly to OpenRouter (#209)

Add a "Discuss" button to each detailed comment on the review page that
opens a popup chat for interrogating that specific comment. The chat
streams directly from the browser to OpenRouter so the user's key never
touches our servers — it lives only in tab-scoped sessionStorage and is
reused via the existing PKCE login / paste-key flow. Model is
user-pickable (reuses ModelPicker), defaulting to the review's model.

Context per turn = full paper markdown (already client-side) + the
comment's quote/feedback + overall review. Since the pipeline's literal
per-comment reasoning isn't persisted, the system prompt reconstructs it
and is instructed to concede when a comment is wrong rather than
manufacture authority.

New: openrouterChat.ts (prompt + SSE transport + tab-scoped persistence),
useOpenRouterKey.ts (key hook + OAuth ?code= exchange), CommentChat.tsx
(popup UI). Wired into ReviewDisplay.tsx. The chat thread + review access
token are stashed in sessionStorage and recovered across the OAuth
redirect (ReviewPageClient) so private reviews survive a dropped ?token=.

Pure web feature — no src/coarse, schema, or Modal changes; no PyPI release.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(deploy,web): persist structured Review JSON and feed it to the comment chat (#209)

The pipeline already builds a structured Review (stable comment numbers,
severity, confidence, structured overall feedback) but only the rendered
markdown was stored, so consumers re-parse markdown and lose
severity/confidence + recommendation/revision-targets.

- Add reviews.result_json (jsonb): migration deploy/migrate_result_json.sql
  (idempotent) + baseline supabase_schema.sql.
- Modal worker writes it for new web reviews via _review_result_json(),
  scrubbing NUL bytes (rejected by jsonb) through _strip_nul_bytes.
- Thread result_json through the review API + web Review type.
- The per-comment chat now passes the comment's severity/confidence and a
  richer structured overall feedback (assessment, recommendation, revision
  targets) when present, falling back to the markdown parse for legacy /
  CLI-handoff reviews (result_json NULL; no backfill).

Groundwork for a future second-round review feature. No src/coarse change,
so no PyPI release. Deploy: apply the migration + redeploy Modal (soak on
preview first). Coverage in tests/test_modal_worker.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): chat model fallback for non-OpenRouter review models; Discuss + status on overall feedback (#209)

Two fixes from local testing:

- The chat sent the review's `model` straight to OpenRouter, but a review run
  via the CLI-handoff/subscription path has a model like
  "coarse-review-cli:codex" (or a bare host model) that OpenRouter rejects.
  normalizeModel() now falls back to a valid OpenRouter default unless the
  model is a "provider/model" ID.

- The Discuss button and the ✓/✕ status controls only appeared on detailed
  comments, not the Overall Feedback ("major") issues. Issues are now
  checkable + discussable at parity: useCommentStatus is keyed by an explicit
  key list, issues use a negative key (no collision with comment numbers) so
  they dim/filter via the same store without inflating the comment "remaining"
  count, and Discuss opens a chat on a synthetic comment (issue body as
  feedback, no quote). The persisted-chat OAuth-restore resolves both comment
  and issue targets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): AI-subscription handoff + disconnect-key control in chat (#209)

- "Discuss with your AI" header menu: bundles the OCR'd paper + the full
  review + the structured review JSON into one markdown file, downloads it,
  copies a kickoff prompt, and opens Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini / Grok /
  DeepSeek in a new tab so users can continue on a subscription they already
  pay for. New web/src/lib/aiHandoff.ts + web/src/components/SubscriptionHandoffMenu.tsx.

- The comment chat now has a "Disconnect key" control next to the model
  picker. The OpenRouter key lives only in tab-scoped sessionStorage, so it
  persists within a tab once entered (the gate only re-appears on a fresh
  tab); disconnecting clears it and re-shows the key gate, which also lets
  users switch keys / accounts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): prefill the AI-subscription handoff prompt via service URL params (#209)

We can't paste into a cross-origin page's input, so prefill the composer using
each service's own URL query param where one exists: ChatGPT, Claude, and Grok
support ?q= (verified current); Gemini and DeepSeek have no native prefill, so
they open clean and rely on the clipboard copy (kept as the universal fallback
for all services). The kickoff prompt is reworded to stay sensible even if a
service auto-submits ?q= before the file is attached. The menu note now tells
the user whether the prompt was prefilled or needs pasting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): add Kimi, Qwen, GLM to the AI-subscription handoff menu (#209)

Three more services at the bottom of the "Discuss with your AI" dropdown.
No known native URL prefill for any of them, so they open clean and rely on
the clipboard copy (same as Gemini/DeepSeek).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pipeline): emit feedback math as LaTeX across all feedback agents (#209)

Math in the overview ("major") comments rendered as plain text because no
prompt told the model to format math as LaTeX — the section agent happened
to emit $...$, the overview agent emitted "E_n[Z_j(...)]"/"alpha_j", which
KaTeX can't render. The renderer/CSS/preprocessLatex are identical for both;
the gap was content.

Add a shared MATH_FORMATTING_GUIDANCE (prompts.py) appended to every feedback
agent's system prompt via a new feedback_system_prompt(base, form) helper,
replacing the bare `base + document_form_notice(form)` in the overview,
section, cross-section, and editorial agents. It instructs the model to wrap
math in $...$ / $$...$$ while leaving verbatim quotes untouched. Applies to
the manuscript path too (no longer byte-identical to the bare base) — the
agent system-prompt tests are updated accordingly.

Affects new reviews only; existing stored reviews are unchanged. Coverage in
tests/test_prompts.py. This is a src/coarse change → ships in the next PyPI
release. Also documents the earlier AI-subscription handoff work in CHANGELOG.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): draggable divider to resize the paper panel on the review page (#209)

PaperPanel takes a width prop (default 45%); ReviewDisplay holds the width as
a % of the body (clamped 20–75, persisted in localStorage) and renders a
col-resize handle between the panel and the review column that drives it via
document-level mousemove/mouseup. The handle is hidden on mobile/print (where
the paper panel is already hidden). Lets users expand the review without
fully hiding the paper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): recover the review access token on any reload, not just the OAuth return (#209)

The earlier recovery only re-hydrated the token from sessionStorage when ?code=
was present (the immediate OAuth callback). So if a token-gated review's URL
lost ?token= (OpenRouter not preserving the query string on redirect) and the
user later reloaded, the fetch 401'd ("Access token required").

ReviewPageClient now stashes the token per review (coarse.review_return_token:<id>)
whenever the URL carries it, and recovers it on ANY token-less load — keyed by
review id so it can't leak across reviews, sessionStorage-scoped so it clears on
tab close. The redundant global stash in useOpenRouterKey.startLogin is removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pre-pr review fixes (#209)

From the pre-PR review agents:
- Extend the LaTeX math-formatting guidance to the remaining feedback agents —
  completeness and proof-verify now route through feedback_system_prompt too, so
  "every feedback agent" is accurate (verify is the most math-heavy). Tests +
  CHANGELOG updated.
- aiHandoff: defer URL.revokeObjectURL past the click tick (revoking synchronously
  can cancel the download in some browsers).
- CommentChat: drop the unused clearPersistedChat import; don't show a "No response"
  error when the user hits Stop (clean abort with empty content isn't a failure).
- migrate_result_json.sql: document the migration-before-deploy ordering (missing
  column 42703's every review write/fetch).
- test_modal_worker: assert the terminal "done" write includes result_json (guards
  the call-site plumbing, not just the helper).

All green: ruff clean, 1041 tests pass, web tsc clean, security scanner clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

recent_commits.message: fix(pipeline): drop section-stage timeout that discarded comments without bounding wall-clock (#208)
recent_commits.body:
* fix(pipeline): drop section-stage timeout that discarded comments without bounding wall-clock

The section-review stage collected per-section futures with
as_completed(section_futures, timeout=_SECTION_STAGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS). On
timeout it relabelled stragglers "Skipped" but never cancelled them; because
the futures were launched inside a `with ThreadPoolExecutor(...)` block, exiting
that block calls shutdown(wait=True), which blocks until every straggler
finishes anyway. So the timeout bounded no wall-clock — it only discarded
comments that were computed and paid for.

Collect every result via plain as_completed(section_futures); keep the
per-future try/except that skips an individual section whose agent raises. The
per-call timeout in the headless client remains the real safety bound. Drop the
unused _SECTION_STAGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS constant, its TimeoutError handler, and
the now-dead skip-unfinished relabel loop.

Tests: a behavioral test (12-section paper > 10-worker pool retains a comment
from every section) plus a structural guard that fails if the stage-level
timeout is reintroduced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(pipeline): pin the per-section failure path in the section-review loop

Pre-PR review (test-engineer) noted the retained per-future try/except — a
failing section agent drops only that section's comment and labels it
"Skipped" — had no direct test after the timeout-test rewrite. Add one: a
3-section paper where section 2's agent raises asserts its comment is excluded
from the editorial input and labelled "Skipped section", while sections 1 and 3
are retained and labelled "Reviewed".

recent_commits.message: fix(web): make Turnstile resilient on Safari + honest failure message (#205) (#206)
recent_commits.body:
The homepage Turnstile effect (web/src/app/page.tsx) treated the first
error-callback as permanently fatal and timed out at 12s with a "blocked
by a browser extension" message — both misfire on Safari, where ITP makes
transient error-callbacks common and challenges often go interactive.

- error-callback now resets + retries up to 2x via turnstile.reset() before
  giving up, instead of dead-ending on the first transient error.
- New before-interactive-callback cancels the silent-block watchdog so an
  interactive challenge awaiting a user click isn't falsely failed; watchdog
  budget raised 12s -> 20s for slow/ITP loads.
- Failure message drops the "extension or hostname mismatch most likely"
  assertion; names the real causes (strict privacy modes incl. Safari ITP,
  content blockers, slow networks), leads with "reload first", keeps the CLI
  fallback.

Server-side verify (web/src/lib/turnstile.ts) unchanged — anti-abuse posture
unaffected. Web-only.

recent_commits.message: fix(cli): fail fast on missing OpenRouter key + silence Windows perm warning (#197, #199) (#201)
recent_commits.body:
#197 — local coarse-review forwarded a literal/blank OpenRouter key
(e.g. `openrouter = "FROM_ENV"` with empty env) as `Bearer FROM_ENV`,
401'd deep in Mistral OCR, and in --detach mode left a zero-byte log +
bare exit 2. Fixes:
- headless_review._find_openrouter_key now accepts only sk-or-… shaped
  values (new _looks_like_openrouter_key), so junk from any source is
  skipped and resolution falls through instead of returning a placeholder.
- cli_review.main() preflights the key before OCR (PDF + not
  --pre-extracted): prints shared _OPENROUTER_KEY_HELP and returns exit 3.
- detached child launched with `python -u`; cli_attach recognizes the
  preflight error as a failure marker so --attach reports exit 1, not 2.

#199 — config.py load_config ran a POSIX st_mode & 0o077 check
unconditionally, warning on every Windows run (NTFS reports emulated
0o666; chmod 600 is a no-op). Now guarded with `if os.name != "nt":`,
mirroring save_config.

Tests: test_headless_review.py, test_cli_review.py, test_cli_attach.py,
test_config.py.

recent_commits.message: fix(pipeline): graceful short-quote handling + editorial cross-comment dedup (#198, #200) (#203)
recent_commits.body:
#198 — a short/bare-LaTeX-token quote (e.g. \cmidrule(lr){2-5}, 18 chars)
failed DetailedComment.quote min_length=20; instructor validates the whole
batch, so one bad quote exhausted retries and made the section agent raise,
which pipeline.py turns into skipping (losing) the entire section. section.py
now parses into a lenient _LooseSectionComment envelope, then SectionAgent.run
drops low-value quotes (<20 chars or bare LaTeX-token runs) and re-validates
survivors as strict DetailedComments — one dropped comment instead of a lost
section. Section prompt also forbids bare LaTeX-token quotes.

#200 — the editorial pass had no cross-comment dedup (that instruction lived
only in the fallback-only crossref prompt) and ran at temp 0.15, so the same
input produced ~80 vs ~18 comments across runs. Adds a MERGE step to the
editorial prompt, drops editorial temperature to 0.0, and applies a soft
_MAX_FINAL_COMMENTS=25 ceiling in run_editorial_pass as a deterministic
backstop.

Tests: test_section.py, test_prompts.py, test_review_stages.py.

+followed (within ~80 non-period chars) by "Cohen('s)? h = <h>". When a

+(`N_source = "binom_n_out_of_N"`) and check.R re-computes the two-sided
+binomial p via `stats::binom.test()` assuming p_null = 0.5 (the most
+common null in binomia

+  # v0.6.2: exact binomial test with Cohen's h effect size.

repos.readme_excerpt:
A metadata and text extraction and text manipulation tool set for the statistical programming language [R](www.r-project.org). 

**JATSdecoder** facilitates text mining projects on scientific articles by enabling an individual selection of metadata and text parts. 
Its function `JATSdecoder()` extracts metadata, sectioned text and reference list from [NISO-JATS](https://jats.nlm.nih.gov/publishing/tag-library/1.1d2/index.html) coded XML files.

repos.readme_excerpt:
Auto Z-Curve reads a folder of PDF articles, uses Google's Gemini AI to extract focal statistical results from each one, and produces a z-curve analysis report — including a plot, summary statistics, and a full disclosure table.

You interact with it through a simple terminal interface: point it at your PDFs, enter your API key, and click Run.

In the below screenshot, I z-curved 45 articles on cognitive dissonance in under 4 minutes, costing under 2 USD in tokens.

![](assets/example.svg)

**Note:** I have validated statistic extraction as part of a study, which is currently under peer review

r_meta.description_text: Provide reproducible R chunks in R Markdown document that automatically check computational results for reproducibility. This is achieved by creating json files storing metadata about computational results. A comprehensive tutorial to the package is available as preprint by Brandmaier & Peikert (2024, <doi:10.31234/osf.io/3zjvf>).

repos.readme_excerpt:
Also read our Open Access publication about this package in _Collabra: Psychology_:
[Automated Reproducibility Testing in R Markdown](https://doi.org/10.1525/collabra.138638)


This package allows you to make computational results in R testable for reproduction (does the same script with the same data produce the same results, e.g. on a different computer and/or later in time). There is only a single thing you need to change in your analysis if you are already using RMarkdown: Load the package at the beginning of your R Markdown file (`library(reproducibleRchunks)`) and change the code chunk t

highlights.message: feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark)
highlights.body:
The dominant defect on two-column APA papers: pdftotext serializes a page's
columns interleaved, scrambling Method/Results/Discussion order and splitting
paragraphs. The Step-1 whole-page corrector (v2.4.82) cannot reach these pages
-- its bilateral y-row gate and full-height gutter strip both reject any page
carrying a full-width band (table row / banner / wide title) crossing the column
centre, which is exactly the failing pages (TRIAGE 2026-06-15: GENERAL=1 was
byte-near-identical on these papers).

Step 2 (extract_page_text_banded, docpluck/extract_columns.py) segments a
flagged page into horizontal y-BANDS: a band whose central gutter strip is
glyph-free is two-column prose -> re-extracted left-then-right; a full-width band
(table / banner / title) is kept as-is. Bands reassemble top-to-bottom at
glyph-free cut lines (vertically-overlapping bands merge to full-width so a tall
title glyph is never bisected). Applied ONLY as a fallback inside
splice_column_corrected_pages when the whole-page path returns "", under the SAME
unconditional word-preservation guard -- a band crop that drops/fabricates a word
is rejected and the page kept as-is, so the flag can only ADD a pure reorder,
never ship corruption.

Gated behind DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_BANDED (default OFF, ship-dark): flag OFF is

highlights.message: test(canary): assert audit input PDF byte-equal to pinned feed (R-0003)
highlights.body:
Cross-project lesson transfer (CitationGuard => docpluck): the verification
substrate must be byte-equal to the production input feed before scoring. A
CitationGuard run once scored its tool output against raw PyMuPDF for a whole
run, producing a misleading score.

docpluck already scores render-vs-AI-gold (never a deterministic extractor --
CLAUDE.md hard rule), so the residual gap that lesson maps to here is INPUT
provenance: a render of a drifted PDF scored against a gold made from the
original is the same "wrong substrate" bug class. If the cached PDF for a DOI
ever drifts (re-download, re-encode, a stray Dropbox conflicted-copy), the
canary would silently score a render of a different input than the gold.

- tools/canary_provenance.py: load_expected_sha() + check_provenance() helpers
- canary.json: pin expected_pdf_sha for all 5 onboarded canary papers
- render_for_audit.py: --expected-sha (falls back to the canary pin); abort with
  exit 4 BEFORE rendering on mismatch; record expected_pdf_sha + provenance_ok
  in the per-render manifest. Un-pinned keys are a no-op (purely additive guard).
- tests/test_canary_provenance.py: 10 tests (helper units, 5 byte-equality
  integration vs the real cached PDFs, render-guard exit-4)

Gate: 10/10 new tests pass; full library suite 1710 passed / 0 failed (no
regression); live render smoke auto-loads the pin, matches, renders, exit 0.
The Sonnet canary itself is not runnable in this env (ai-gold get --view reading
errors + claude -p nesting hang); provenance is a pre-render guard that does not
change render output, so pytest is the precise gate.

highlights.message: stat_effect_size: detect tests via extract_eq(); add implied sample sizes (#308)
highlights.body:
Replace the module's duplicate t/F/effect-size regex extraction with the
shared extract_eq() equation table, per #308. Detection now reads tests and
effect sizes from eq rows (one output row per test), removing ~315 lines of
duplicated extraction: the two text_search detection pipelines, the
expand_multi_*_rows helpers, and the now-unreachable multi-test window
branches in the coherence classifiers.

Also fixes the #308 warnings (early return when no tests are detected) and,
validated against the coded ground truth (0.0.1.0):
- F-tests again require a two-integer (df1, df2), matching the validated
  detection; bare "t = value" is kept as before.
- test_text no longer glues a literal "NA" when df is absent.

For d coherence matches, the coherence note now reports the sample sizes that
reproduce the reported effect size (n1 = n2, N for equal-n; closest n1, n2, N
for unequal-n) so users can manually verify; a d_implied_n column is added to
the table. Paired dz records n = df + 1 without extra note text.

Corpus parity at the individual-test level: 2257/2281 coded tests retained;
remaining differences are mostly fuller value parsing and correct row
splitting. Ground truth needs regenerating/recoding for the new row structure.

highlights.message: fix(v2.4.84): R2 quantifier-head guard — stop deleting digits from reference titles
highlights.body:
CitationGuard handoff 2026-06-10 item 4: plos_med_1 "Clinimetric properties of
3 instruments" rendered as "of instruments" — the standalone "3" was silently
deleted. Root cause is docpluck's own R2 page-number scrub (normalize.py), NOT
pdftotext (raw extracts "of 3 instruments" correctly): R2 strips any digit whose
value also appears as a standalone page-number line when it sits between two
lowercase words. "3" is page 3, so the quantifier "3 instruments" was stripped.
The v2.4.17 body-noun allowlist never enumerated "instruments" — whack-a-mole.

Fix keys on a closed class instead (NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.30 -> 1.9.31): a
genuine page leak follows a CONTENT word ("psychological 41 science"); a
legitimate quantifier follows a FUNCTION word ("of 3 instruments", "the 5
factors", "and 3 in"). _r2_is_body_phrase now also preserves a digit whose
immediately-preceding token is in _R2_QUANTIFIER_HEAD_WORDS. Purely additive —
only ever preserves, never strips more — honoring the rule-0a asymmetry
(dropping a digit from a reference title is far worse than a stray page number).

Corpus audit (101 PDFs, academic-normalized, old vs new): 5 papers changed, all
correct restorations, 0 regressions. Repaired the filed defect PLUS 4
pre-existing silent drops: amp_1 "12 academic search engines", bmc_med_3 "5
types", ieee_access_5 "3 x 3" + "30 iterations", maier_2023 "Experiments 1a and
3". The 11 remaining R2 strips (all content-word-preceded) are genuine leaks,
correctly still stripped.

Handoff items 1-3 (chen_2021_jesp: lost "Registered reports:" prefix, "TurkPrime.
Com" space injection, "Open, S. C." author mangle) are WON'T-FIX: verified
byte-identical in BOTH pdftotext and pdfplumber -> PDF-embedded-text-layer
corruption, no signal to trigger a fallback, no general signature, tool-swap
forbidden (L-001/AGPL). Established project_citationguard_extraction_defects_wontfix class.

Tests: quantifier-head helper positives/negative + real-PDF plos_med_1 assertion.
37 normalize/idempotency + 299 references/render/normalize + 187 normalization
tests pass.

SKIP_CANARY=1 justification: the pre-commit canary hook could not run at all —
`claude` CLI is absent from PATH (infra failure, not a finding). Deterministic
canary evidence supplied instead: the 101-PDF old-vs-new academic-normalized diff
shows only 5 correct restorations and 0 regressions; render.py is untouched.

digest.narrative: Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. Notably, the `scienceverse/metacheck` project has been actively updated, with merges from the `dev` branch to the `main` branch, including the addition of a Zenodo citation. Meanwhile, `giladfeldman/docpluck` has released version "v2.4.90", featuring a new "per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction" feature, and `giladfeldman/escicheck` has been synced with the latest versions of effectcheck. Additionally, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` has added a CodeQL analysis workflow configuration, and `JamieCummins/regcheck` has improved report viewing and added quality of life features. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, with projects like `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` also making strides, revising validation notes and including citations to further enhance research tools. Overall, it's an exciting time for our community, with many projects pushing forward and building upon each other's progress.
